title: Get Sleep Log by Date
description:
This endpoint returns a list of a user's sleep log entries for a given date. The data returned can include sleep periods that began on the previous date. For example, if you request a Sleep Log for 2021-12-22, it may return a log entry that began the previous night on 2021-12-21, but ended on 2021-12-22.

It uses units that correspond to the Accept-Language header provided.

parameters.name: user-id
parameters.description: The encoded ID of the user. Use "-" (dash) for current logged-in user.

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: The date for the sleep log to be returned in the format yyyy-MM-dd.

response_fields.name: sleep : dateOfSleep
response_fields.description: The date the sleep log ended

response_fields.name: sleep : duration
response_fields.description: Length of the sleep in milliseconds.

response_fields.name: sleep : efficiency
response_fields.description: Calculated sleep efficiency score. See Sleep Efficiency Calculation .

response_fields.name: sleep : endTime
response_fields.description: Time the sleep log ended.

response_fields.name: sleep : infoCode
response_fields.description: An integer value representing the quality of data collected within the sleep log. 0 = Sufficient data to generate a sleep log. 1 = Insufficient heart rate data. 2 = Sleep period was too short (less than 3 hours). 3 = Server-side issue when generating the sleep log. No data impact.

response_fields.name: sleep : isMainSleep
response_fields.description: Boolean value: true or false

response_fields.name: sleep : levels : data : dateTime
response_fields.description: Timestamp the user started in sleep level.

response_fields.name: sleep : levels : data : level
response_fields.description: The sleep level the user entered. The values returned for the sleep log type are: classic : restless | asleep | awake stages : deep | light | rem | wake

response_fields.name: sleep : levels : data : seconds
response_fields.description: The length of time the user was in the sleep level. Displayed in seconds.

response_fields.name: sleep : levels : shortData : dateTime
response_fields.description: Timestamp the user started in sleep level. Only supported when log type = stages.

response_fields.name: sleep : levels : shortData : level
response_fields.description: The sleep level the user entered. Only supported when log type = stages.

response_fields.name: sleep : levels : shortData : seconds
response_fields.description: The length of time the user was in the sleep level. Displayed in seconds.

response_fields.name: sleep : levels : summary : [level] : count
response_fields.description: Total number of times the user entered the sleep level.

response_fields.name: sleep : levels : summary : [level] : minutes
response_fields.description: Total number of minutes the user appeared in the sleep level.

response_fields.name: sleep : levels : summary : [level] : thirtyDayAvgMinutes
response_fields.description: The average sleep stage time over the past 30 days. A sleep stage log is required to generate this value. When a classic sleep log is recorded, this value will be missing.

response_fields.name: sleep : logId
response_fields.description: Sleep log ID.

response_fields.name: sleep : minutesAfterWakeup
response_fields.description: The total number of minutes after the user woke up.

response_fields.name: sleep : minutesAsleep
response_fields.description: The total number of minutes the user was asleep.

response_fields.name: sleep : minutesAwake
response_fields.description: The total sum of "wake" minutes only. It does not include before falling asleep or after waking up.

response_fields.name: sleep : minutesToFallAsleep
response_fields.description: The total number of minutes before the user falls asleep. This value is generally 0 for autosleep created sleep logs.

response_fields.name: sleep : logType
response_fields.description: The type of sleep in terms of how it was logged. See "logType" for more detail. Supported : auto_detected | manual

response_fields.name: sleep : startTime
response_fields.description: Time the sleep log begins.

response_fields.name: sleep : timeInBed
response_fields.description: Total number of minutes the user was in bed.

response_fields.name: sleep : type
response_fields.description: The type of sleep log. Supported : classic | stages

response_fields.name: summary : totalMinutesAsleep
response_fields.description: Total number of minutes the user was asleep across all sleep records in the sleep log.

response_fields.name: summary : totalSleepRecords
response_fields.description: The number of sleep records within the sleep log.

response_fields.name: summary : totalTimeInBed
response_fields.description: Total number of minutes the user was in bed across all records in the sleep log.
